All Questions
Were You Asked This?
Design a photo search application

Design a mobile photo application that allows users to search for photos using keywords and retrieve all images containing those elements (e.g., searching for 'mountains' returns all photos with mountains). Focus on designing the backend system to support this functionality.

Asked at:

Google

A photo search application is a cloud-backed service (think Google Photos or Apple Photos) that automatically analyzes your images, assigns machine-generated tags like “mountain,” “beach,” or “dog,” and lets you instantly retrieve matching photos by typing keywords or short natural-language queries. Users primarily upload/sync photos from their phones, then rely on a fast, reliable search to surface exactly the images they want.

Interviewers ask this question to see if you can design a real-world ML-enabled search system end-to-end: object storage for large media, asynchronous computer-vision pipelines, inverted/vector indexing, relevance/ranking, ACL-aware search, and cost-aware scale-out. Strong candidates balance latency and consistency, choose the right data models and indexes, and reason about multi-tenant privacy, operations, and growth.

Community Solutions
Design a photo search application
by SmallHarlequinMarsupial210
2
11
Design a photo search application
by Jujabi
0
1
Common Functional Requirements

Most candidates end up covering this set of core functionalities

Users should be able to upload and sync photos from mobile devices to the cloud.

Users should be able to search their photo library by keywords or short natural-language queries (e.g., “mountains,” “golden retriever”).

Users should be able to view relevant results quickly with pagination and simple filters (e.g., time range, location).

Users should be able to keep photos private or share albums, and search results should respect those permissions.

Common Deep Dives

Common follow-up questions interviewers like to ask for this question

How will you design the ML-powered indexing pipeline to detect objects, scenes, and generate searchable tags/embeddings at upload time?

How can you achieve consistently low latency (e.g., <200 ms P50) for keyword search and result fetching at scale?

How will you keep storage growth and costs under control for billions of photos?

How do you enforce privacy and sharing permissions in the search index without leaking data?

Relevant Patterns

Relevant patterns that you should know for this question

Handling Large Blobs

Photos are large binary objects that should live in object storage with CDNs and lifecycle policies. Handling uploads, multi-size thumbnails, and bandwidth optimization is central to the design.

Read More
Managing Long Running Tasks

Computer vision inference, embedding generation, and reindexing are asynchronous, potentially expensive jobs. A background pipeline with queues, retries, and idempotency is essential.

Read More
Scaling Reads

Search is read-dominant and latency sensitive. You need search engine sharding/replication, caching, and pagination strategies to serve low-latency queries at scale.

Read More
Relevant Technologies

Relevant technologies that could be used to solve this question

Elasticsearch

Elasticsearch provides inverted indexes, analyzers, synonyms, and optional vector search—ideal for keyword-based photo search with fast pagination and relevance tuning.

Read More
Kafka

Kafka cleanly decouples uploads from ML processing and reindexing. It absorbs spikes, guarantees ordering per key (e.g., photo ID), and supports reliable backfills and model upgrades.

Read More
DynamoDB

DynamoDB scales for metadata and ACLs with predictable latency, high write throughput from ingestion, and flexible key designs for per-user queries without managing complex ops.

Read More
Similar Problems to Practice

Related problems to practice for this question

Instagram

Both manage massive photo libraries, large-blob storage, metadata/thumbnail pipelines, and need fast discovery features; while Instagram is social-first, the media handling patterns overlap.

Read
Practice
FB Post Search

Designing fast, relevance-aware search over user-generated content with per-user permissions and caching is directly analogous, though the content is text vs. photo tags.

Read
Practice
Dropbox

Large file storage, sync, deduplication, and lifecycle management are shared challenges; adding search over metadata parallels building indexes on files/folders.

Read
Practice
Red Flags to Avoid

Common mistakes that can sink candidates in an interview

Running ML inference synchronously on the upload/write path

This inflates p99 upload latency, amplifies timeouts under load, and couples availability of the search pipeline to core data writes. It should be offloaded to an asynchronous, resilient pipeline.

Ignoring ACLs in the search index and filtering only after retrieval

Post-filtering large result sets is slow and risks data leaks via caches, timing, or partial results. ACLs must be enforced at index or query time with strong isolation.

Storing photos as blobs in a relational database without a CDN or lifecycle

Databases are ill-suited for large media; costs and IO will explode and throughput will collapse. Use object storage, CDNs, and lifecycle/tiers to control cost and latency.
